options: I and either II or IV follow

questions.question_text: A graph showing all the combinations of goods and services that can be produced if all of society's resources are used efficiently is a
Suppose the demand for good Z goes up when the price of good Y goes down. We can say that goods Z and Y are

options: a fall in the price of a complementary good.
a fall in the number of substitute goods.

questions.question_text: All of the following can be classified as positive economics except:
options: In times of depression, unemployment rises.
Rise in tax rates increases the revenue of the government.
Sharp decline in industrial output may hurt the GDP growth rate.
Increase in petrol prices leads to increase in demand for CNG vehicles.

questions.question_text: Given that the market for shirts is in equilibrium. If the price of cotton decreases, the equilibrium price and quantity (respectively) of shirts will:
Which of the following is not a macroeconomic issue?
options: The study of the relationship between the unemployment rate and the inflation rate
The forces determining the price level in an individual market
The determination of total output in the economy
The aggregate behavior of all decision-making units in the economy

questions.question_text: France experiences an improvement in productivity due to the introduction of improved technology. In terms of France's production possibility frontier, we would show this change as
options: a shift from a point outside the frontier to a point inside the frontier.
a shift from a point on the frontier to a point outside the frontier.
a shift outwards by the entire frontier.

questions.question_text: Energizer batteries and Duracell's batteries are substitutes. The Energizer cuts supply and increases the price of its batteries. For Duracell, equilibrium price will and equilibrium quantity will in the market.
The demand for JIF peanut butter will decrease, if there is an increase in
options: the price of JIF peanut butter.
the price of Peter Pan peanut butter (a substitute).

questions.question_text: A 10% increase in the price of video games results in a 5% decrease in the quantity of video games demanded. The price elasticity of demand is and demand is and total revenue will
When an economy uses all its resources fully in production then;
options: It will always lie on the PPF
It will lie outside the PPF
It may not lie on the PPF
It will lie on the mid-point of the PPF

questions.question_text: Chicken and fish are substitutes. If the price of chicken increases, the demand for fish will;
options: increase or decrease but the demand curve for chicken will not change.
increase and the demand curve for fish will shift rightward.
not change but there will be a movement along the demand curve for fish.
decrease and the demand curve for fish will shift leftward

questions.question_text: The price of a bus rides decreases, but the total revenue of the bus company decreases. The demand for bus rides is
The simple Keynesian model
options: overstated the effect of an increase in government spending by neglecting the necessary increase in the interest rate and consequent decline in investment that accompany an increase in government spending.

understated the effect of an increase in government spending by neglecting the necessary increase in the interest rate and consequent decline in investment that accompany an increase in government spending.

overstated the effect of an increase in government spending by neglecting the necessary decrease in the interest rate and consequent increase in investment that accompany a decrease in government spending.

understated the effect of an increase in government spending by neglecting the necessary decrease in the interest rate and consequent decrease in investment that accompany an increase in government spending.

questions.question_text: When moving from gross domestic product to gross national product, one has to
options: subtract earnings by foreign residents and firms in India.
subtract foreign earnings of Indian residents and firms.
add foreign earnings of Indian residents and firms.

questions.question_text: In the simple Keynesian model, if the equilibrium level of income is Rs. 300 billion, the MPC is 0.75, and government expenditures increase by 20 billion. What is the new equilibrium level of income?
In the liquidity trap case where the LM schedule is nearly horizontal,
options: both monetary and fiscal policy are highly effective.
monetary and fiscal policy are ineffective.
monetary policy is ineffective and fiscal policy is effective.
fiscal policy is ineffective and monetary policy is effective.

questions.question_text: Monetary policy will be
options: less effective the higher the interest elasticity of investment, and thus the steeper the IS schedule.
more effective the higher the interest elasticity of investment, and thus the flatter the IS schedule.
equally effective regardless of whether or not the interest elasticity of investment is higher or lower, or the IS curve is flatter or steeper.
more effective with a vertical IS curve.

questions.question_text: The main decision for a profit maximizing perfectly competitive firm is not what ______ but what ______
options: level of output to produce; price to charge
price to charge; level of output to produce
level of output to produce; total revenue to achieve
price to charge; total cost to achieve

questions.question_text: Molly the Monopolist faces an elastic demand curve. If she decreases price, marginal revenue will be and total revenue will.
Consumer surplus is:
options: equal to price minus marginal cost
less in the case of single price monopoly than in the case of a perfectly competitive industry
positive in the case of a monopolist practicing perfect price discrimination

questions.question_text: Compared to a competitive industry, a monopoly transfers:
options: deadweight loss away from producers to consumers
deadweight loss away from consumers to producers

questions.question_text: Firms in an oligopoly: i. are independent of each other's actions, ii. can each influence the market price, iii. charge a price equal
Opportunity cost, most broadly define, is
options: the additional cost of producing an additional unit of output.
what we forgo, or give up, when we make a choice or a decision.
a cost that cannot be avoided, regardless of what is done in the future.
the additional cost of buying an additional unit of a product.

questions.question_text: The accelerator theory of investment says that induced investment is determined by
options: the rate of change of national income.

questions.question_text: As the MPS increases, the multiplier will
options: either increase or decrease depending on the size of the change in investment.

questions.question_text: For which of the following market structures is it assumed that there are barriers to entry?
A market with few entry barriers and with many firms that sell differentiated products is:
A Giffen good:
options: Is a good that people buy more of as their incomes fall.
Is a good which people buy more of as its price increases.
Is a good on which people spend a small portion of their income.

questions.question_text: Fiscal policy refers to:
options: The actions of the central bank in controlling the money supply.
The spending and taxing policies used by the government to influence the economy.
The government's regulation of financial intermediaries.

questions.question_text: An increase in the demand of the imported commodity subject to a given import quota;
options: reduces domestic quantity demanded of the commodity
increases the domestic production of the commodity
reduces domestic price of the commodity

questions.question_text: If a nation gains from trade, its consumption point is
A country possesses a comparative advantage in the production of a product if
options: the opportunity cost, in terms of the amount of other products that it gives up to produce this product, is lower than it is for its trading partners.
it possesses an absolute advantage in the production of this good compared to its trading partners.
it is able to produce less of this good per worker than its trading partners.
it can produce more of this good per hour than its trading partners.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a proposition of the Heckscher-Ohlin model?
options: A country has a comparative advantage in the production of that commodity which uses more intensively the country's more abundant resource.
The effect of international trade is to tend to equalize factor prices between the trading nations.
If the United States is a skilled labor abundant country, then the United States has a comparative advantage in the production of goods that use skilled labor more intensively.
Countries will completely specialize in the product in which they have a comparative advantage if free trade is allowed to occur.

questions.question_text: The Heckscher - Ohlin trade theory explains most of the trade;
We say that commodity Y is K-intensive with respect to X when:
options: More K is used in the production of Y than X
Less L is used in the production of Y than X
A lower L/K ratio is used in the production of Y than X
A higher K/L is used in the production of X than Y
